Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, elevated creatinine (suggesting impaired renal function), and mild hyperchloremia. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings suggest possible renal dysfunction with tubular involvement and impaired glucose handling; further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.
